title: NA-Non ULIP

International Youth Day 2023: Why Buy Life Insurance Plan Early On?
India stands in the fifth position accounting for countries with large youth populations in the world1. As per the 2011 Census, more than 58% of the Indian population is below the age of 29 years2. The younger population is one of the valuable assets to any country. In order to recognise the potential of the younger population for today’s society across the country and the globe, International Youth Day is celebrated on 12th August every year. Specifically, for working-age youths, the professional journey begins with responsibilities. The primary responsibility is to ensure the future financial needs of your family, loved ones, or dependents are secured with an adequate amount of life insurance coverage before starting the financial planning. This International Youth Day, celebrate the day by securing your loved ones.
There is a common misconception among the younger generation that insurance coverage is for older people. It is extremely important for the younger generation to understand the importance of buying a life insurance plan at an early age to reap the maximum benefits. There is a variety of life insurance products available in the market. You can buy a pure protection plan, or you can also invest in a life insurance plan that offers you the dual benefit of life cover and investment for future goals. Be it any type of life insurance plan, investing early has the maximum benefits in many ways. Let us look at the benefits of buying life insurance plans early on.
Benefits of buying a Life Insurance Plan early on:
The following are the benefits of buying a life insurance plan early on:
Longer and higher coverage:
As an early buyer of life insurance, you would get covered for a longer duration. Usually, life insurance coverage is availed till the age of your retirement. If you are buying at 25 years of age, you can avail of coverage for a longer term than when you buy at 35 years of age. Also, with good health, you can get higher coverage at an affordable amount of premium. Insurance companies do not ask you for medical check-ups for many life insurance plans when you buy at an early age. You can easily buy life insurance plans online without any hassles.
Lower cost:
The life insurance premium is calculated based on various factors such as your age, health history, smoking habit, alcohol consumption, family’s medical history, policy tenure, lifestyle, etc. Owing to the low risk of mortality, life insurance premium rates are relatively lower for young people who are fit and in good health. Let us look at the premium rates for individuals of different ages.
Let us check the premium for the term insurance plan with a sum assured of INR 50,00,000 for 25 years term. Let us assume that the insured is a non-smoker and healthy. Here are the premium rates:
The above rates are only for illustrative purposes. The rate may vary from one individual to another benefit. Life insurance plans give you tax benefits. You can avail of the tax deduction on the yearly premium of up to INR 1.5 lacs under section 80C of the Income Tax Act,1961. You could also avail of tax exemptions under Section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, on the lump sum payouts that you receive3. However, you need to keep certain things in mind while buying life insurance plans at an early age, such as choosing the right amount of coverage, policy term, and the additional benefits/riders that are necessary for you based on your insurance needs. Ensure to check the claim settlement ratio of the insurer before you buy. This international youth day, consider buying a life insurance plan suitable for you to secure your loved ones depending on the parameters and the information provided. Availing the riders would also cost an additional amount of premium. However, it is important to understand that the premium remains the same throughout the policy period. Hence, buying life insurance at an early age benefits you with lower costs and longer coverage against eventualities. Premium savings can be utilised for investments.
Power of compounding:
Time is power when it comes to investment. When you are investing in life insurance plans like endowment policies or unit-linked investment plans that come with investment elements along with life protection, it is important to start investing early. These products are designed for your long-term goals. The longer you stay invested, the higher would be the return and the benefit that you receive on maturity. The power of compounding does its magic in this investment. Hence, it is always better to buy life insurance early, be it for protection or for wealth creation.
International Youth Day 2023 is a day to start your financial journey towards building a healthy portfolio for your future. A work begun is half done. Thus, it is important for you to plan your financial future early so that you can reap the benefits when you need them.
